title: Escape skill tooltip rendering for imported skill names

title: Resolve Decapod container workspace validation mismatch
description:
Decapod validate fails on claim.git.container_workspace_required even from an isolated Decapod worktree after decapod docs override --force, decapod workspace ensure --container, and a Podman-backed validation attempt. Existing repo override says containerized proof is not required for routine static HTML validation, but Decapod still enforces the container-workspace gate. Fix by making the override machine-enforced, adding a Decapod-supported bypass, or updating validation policy. Context: spawned from mythras-chargen-p4v5 while documenting source authority and agent workflow ADRs.

## Required Skills
decapod,beads
acceptance_criteria: decapod validate can pass for routine docs/app work in an isolated Decapod worktree, or the repo has a Decapod-supported explicit exception that does not require agents to claim false validation success.
notes:
2026-05-21 Copilot investigation: read AGENTS.md and .decapod/OVERRIDE.md on root/orchestrator branch; claimed with bd. decapod 0.48.6 workspace status in orchestrator branch reports can_work=true, docker_available=true, in_container=false. decapod validate -v --format json still fails only container_workspace_required: 'Not running in container workspace - git-tracked work must execute in Docker-isolated workspace (claim.git.container_workspace_required)'. decapod docs override --force caches OVERRIDE.md but does not affect the validator. decapod docs show interfaces/CLAIMS.md includes the project override row for claim.git.container_workspace_required, but embedded specs/GIT.md and context.scope still mandate container workspaces. decapod workspace ensure --container is not usable under the repo contract because it fails WORKSPACE_NO_CLAIMED_TODO and asks for decapod todo claim; project rules require Beads and prohibit decapod todo. No supported config/capability found for a Beads-backed or static-app exception; leaving open rather than claiming false validation success.
Final audit: Decapod validate still fails only container_workspace_required. decapod workspace ensure --container remains blocked by WORKSPACE_NO_CLAIMED_TODO, which conflicts with the active project override that Beads supersede decapod todo operations. This is recorded as a Decapod/control-plane mismatch, not an app implementation failure.
Upstream bug filed for the Decapod control-plane deadlock: https://github.com/DecapodLabs/decapod/issues/568. Local state remains blocked until Decapod provides a non-decapod-todo container remediation path or override-aware validation.

title: Implement RAW Mythras Core Mysticism magic system

Implement Mysticism as the fifth magic system alongside Rune Magic, Folk Magic, Animism, and Sorcery. Source authority: ADR-0006 accepts all five Mythras magic systems and states Mythras Core is the source for Mysticism; references/mythras-raw/mysticism.json is the RAW Mythras Core extraction/verification surface. Scope should determine how Mysticism is accessed in chargen (cult, career, culture, or no current source-backed access), then expose/apply it only where source/product data supports it. Use TDD: first add public harness coverage for Mysticism source data and any supported access path, then implement minimal app/UI/PDF behavior. Follow AGENTS.md and .decapod/OVERRIDE.md: Beads only, no decapod todo, isolated Decapod worktree, source chain PDF -> references JSON -> inline/UI, no source PDFs committed. Use /lfg or ce-work pipeline, then ce-simplify, ce-code-simplicity-reviewer, ce-correctness-reviewer, and compound-engineering:ce-adversarial-reviewer. title: fix: Correct ~400 garbled OCR entries across all cult MIRACLES_DATA, reference JSONs, and inline constants
notes:
## OCR Garbage Cleanup Plan

### Scope
~400 entries flagged with `split_from_garbled: true` and/or `rune_inferred: true` across
the MIRACLES_DATA inline constant in index.html. These are OCR artifacts from cult one-pager
PDF extractions.

### Examples Found
- "gow Fear 4 Summon Lune" → should be "Fear" + "Summon Lune" (fixed for Jakaleel)
- "o Summon Shade" → should be "Summon Shade"
- "Behold" → not a real miracle (OCR noise)
- Prefixes like "gow", "o", "R", "RS", "jo", "j", "i" are OCR-garbled rune abbreviations

### Approach
1. For each cult with garbled entries, compare inline data against:
   - The per-cult reference JSON (references/cults-raw/<pantheon>/<cult>.json)
   - The source PDF if available (references/cults-upstream/)
2. Extract correct miracle names from reference JSON cultMiracles field
3. Replace garbled inline entries with verified data
4. Remove split_from_garbled/rune_inferred flags from corrected entries
5. Vision-mode verify against source PDF pages where uncertain

### ADR-003 Compliance
All corrections must trace to the reference JSON. If the reference JSON itself
is garbled, fix it first against the source PDF with vision-mode verification.

### Scale
- Total garbled entries: ~400 (grep -c "split_from_garbled" index.html)
- Affected cults: most non-common cults (Storm, Lunar, Praxian, Darkness pantheons)
- Already fixed: 7 Mothers - Jakaleel (commit 4cd024e)
close_reason: Closed

title: feat: Agent-browser-first API refactor — expose App.agent.getState(), granular actions, assertion helpers
notes:
## Agent-Browser-First Refactor Plan

### Goal
Make index.html natively automatable by agent-browser without DOM scraping or fragile eval expressions.

### Phase 1: State Query API
Add read-only API methods that return structured JSON:
- App.agent.getState() -> Full CharacterData + computed attributes
- App.agent.getUIState() -> {currentStep, visiblePickers, errors, toasts}
- App.agent.getValidation() -> {valid: bool, errors: [...], warnings: [...]}
- App.agent.getMagicState() -> {cultType, devotionalPool, boundSpiritSlots, sorceryResource, selectedMiracles, selectedSpells, selectedSpirits}

### Phase 2: Granular Action API
Add mutation methods that return success/error:
- App.agent.setStep(n) -> navigate to step
- App.agent.selectCult(name) -> select cult, trigger picker rendering
- App.agent.toggleSpell(name) -> toggle sorcery spell
- App.agent.toggleSpirit(name) -> toggle bound spirit
- App.agent.selectMiracle(name) -> select/deselect miracle
- App.agent.allocateSkill(name, points) -> allocate points to a skill
- App.agent.setCharacteristic(name, value) -> set a single characteristic

### Phase 3: Assertion Helpers
Add self-checking methods for automated testing:
- App.agent.assertSpellCount() -> {selected, limit, spells}
- App.agent.assertSpiritSlots() -> {used, limit, spirits}
- App.agent.assertMiracles() -> {selected, available, miracles}
- App.agent.assertPlayMode() -> {sections, errors, missing}
- App.agent.assertPDFExport() -> {success, errors}

### Phase 4: Event Log
- App.agent.getEventLog() -> [{action, timestamp, result, error}, ...]
- Auto-records all agent API calls with results

### Success Criteria
- All 30 existing E2E assertions still pass
- New API methods documented in .rpiv/guidance/agent-api.md
- agent-browser eval expressions become one-liners instead of multi-line IIFEs
- No DOM parsing needed for any test assertion

### Estimated Scope
- ~200 lines of new JS in index.html
- Update test-agent-api.mjs to use new API
- Update .rpiv/guidance/agent-api.md
close_reason: Closed
